headline: Key Industry Players Focus on Advancing Computational Storage Solutions
summary: The computational storage market is set to surge from USD 0.93 billion in 2026 to USD 4.30 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 29.0%. Driven by the need for efficient data processing near storage, computational storage supports the growing AI, big data, and high-performance computing demands. Key growth areas include the programmable computational storage segment, offering customizable data processing, and the enterprise storage segment, capitalizing on data center adoptions. headline: How Nvidia Became a Chip Stock Loser—and Why It Might Stay That Way
summary: Nvidia  stock was edging up early Tuesday at the end of an underwhelming first half of the year.  The chip maker is looking to its next-generation hardware to spark a rally but gains might be muted for a while yet.  The major issue for Nvidia is that the huge spending on AI chips is now being spread out among a wider range of semiconductor companies.

content.title: How Nvidia Became a Chip Stock Loser—and Why It Might Stay That Way

The major issue for Nvidia is that the huge spending on AI chips is now being spread out among a wider range of semiconductor companies.

headline: Mizuho lifts TSMC CoWoS capacity forecasts as server CPU demand surges
summary: Investing.com -- Mizuho Securities Asia raised its forecast for TSMC's monthly CoWoS packaging capacity to 140,000 units by 2026 and 190,000–200,000 units by 2027, up from prior estimates of 120,000 and 170,000–180,000 respectively, as a sharply upgraded outlook for AI-driven server CPU demand forces a broad revision to the firm's semiconductor supply model.

content.title: Mizuho lifts TSMC CoWoS capacity forecasts as server CPU demand surges
content.summary: Investing.com -- Mizuho Securities Asia raised its forecast for TSMC's monthly CoWoS packaging capacity to 140,000 units by 2026 and 190,000–200,000 units by 2027, up from prior estimates of 120,000 and 170,000–180,000 respectively, as a sharply upgraded outlook for AI-driven server CPU demand forces a broad revision to the firm's semiconductor supply model.

headline: Intel (INTC) And ASUS Unveil AI Classroom Devices For K 12 Schools
summary: Intel (NasdaqGS:INTC) and ASUS jointly showcased a full lineup of AI focused education devices at ISTE+ASCD 2026. The portfolio includes rugged laptops, desktops, Chromebooks and AI acceleration hardware built around Intel Core Ultra processors. The solutions target K-12 schools and educators, with an emphasis on classroom use, privacy and edge computing. Intel is using this education focused launch to extend its AI hardware push beyond data centers and consumer PCs into classrooms. For...
